tables.title: GI Bleeding Presentations
rows: Vomiting blood, may look like coffee grounds if partially digested
Typically lower GI (brisk upper GI bleed can also present this way)

tables.title: General Medical Red Flags — 'Sicker Than They Look'
rows: Can signal a wide range of serious underlying causes regardless of chief complaint
Vital signs out of proportion to apparent severity
Tachycardia/tachypnea disproportionate to the complaint suggests a more serious process
Pain out of proportion to exam findings
Classic marker for conditions like mesenteric ischemia or compartment syndrome
Inability to find a comfortable position
Contrasts with peritoneal irritation (patient lies still); seen in renal colic and other conditions
A chronic-condition patient's own comparison to their baseline is a valuable, often-overlooked signal
